# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.134.0/http/server.ts";

serve(async (req) => {
  const url = new URL(req.url, req.headers.get("host") || `http://${req.headers.get("x-forwarded-host")}`);
  
  let modulePath: string | undefined;

  // Check for environment variable first
  if (Deno.env.has("MODULE_PATH")) {
    modulePath = Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH");
  } else {
    // If not found in environment, check query parameters
    const queryParams = new URLSearchParams(url.search);
    modulePath = queryParams.get("modulePath");
  }

  if (!modulePath) {
    return new Response("No module path provided", { status: 400 });
  }

  try {
    const mod = await import(modulePath);
    // Assuming the module exports a default function to execute
    if (typeof mod.default === "function") {
      const result = await mod.default();
      return new Response(JSON.stringify(result), { headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } });
    } else {
      throw new Error("Module does not export a default function");
    }
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(`Error loading module: ${error.message}`, { status: 500 });
  }
});
